message:
tests: regenerate cve_arena / release / runtime_math report artifacts

Companion artifact regeneration for the conformance regen in the
previous commit. Every committed JSON / trace / stderr / stdout
under `tests/cve_arena/results/`, `tests/release/`, and
`tests/runtime_math/` is rewritten from scratch by the per-bead
CVE harness, the dossier validator, and the runtime-math
admission-gate / controller-ablation runners, respectively.

`tests/cve_arena/results/` includes per-bead result folders
(`bd-13ya`, `bd-146t`, `bd-15n.2`, `bd-18qq.4`, `bd-1f35`, `bd-1qy`,
`bd-24b6`, `bd-24ug`, `bd-2xp3`, `bd-ahjd`) plus top-level
validation v1.json rollups
(`corpus_normalization`, `format_string_validation`,
`hardened_assertions`, `heap_overflow_validation`,
`integer_overflow_validation`, `paired_mode_evidence`,
`uaf_validation`). Each per-bead folder carries an
`artifact_index.json` pointer set, a
`trace.jsonl` execution trace, sometimes a per-mode
`report.json`, and the hardened / strict `stdout.txt` +
`stderr.txt` pair for each scenario.

What actually changed in the re-emitted files, in bulk:

- **provenance timestamps** roll forward to 2026-04-24 and the
  commit-sha header now points at the ancestor that drove the
  regen (the post-fixture-refactor tree).
- **pthread mutex scenario outputs** (bd-1qy) carry ~6 extra
  stdout lines each under hardened mode because the condvar
  relock fix in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/cond.rs`
  now reports the contention-tracking counters it exposes
  through that harness; this is additive telemetry, no
  assertions changed.
- **bd-1f35 thread lifecycle scenarios** have
  slightly different stderr line ordering after the contended
  cond_timedwait path stopped oscillating state bits; the
  ordering is still deterministic within each scenario so the
  goldens remain exact-match.
- **admission_gate_report.v1.json**, **controller_ablation_report.v1.json**,
  **controller_manifest.v1.json** under `runtime_math/` pick up
  the renamed top-level keys described in the scripts commit
  (`closure.per_epic_breakdown` replacing `closure_per_epic`,
  etc.). Values are unchanged; only the field layout moves.
- **dossier_validation_report.v1.json** under `tests/release/`
  has 131 lines of diff churn, all of which is schema
  rename + timestamp; no scenario gained or lost coverage.

None of these artifact changes relax any assertion; the tests
that consume them have been updated in the harness commit to
read the new layout. Checking them in here keeps the tree
self-consistent (harness test reads new layout ↔ artifact is in
new layout) so `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness` stays green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
